name: Pho (Viet Beef Soup)
recipeIngredient: 4 lb Beef shin with bones or 2 medium oxtails
1/2 lb Leftover cooked sirloin (or beef tenderloin or eye chuck), thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil the beef (with bones) or oxtails in 3 quarts cold water.
2. Skim off foam and fat. Cover and simmer for 4 hours.
3. Broil onion until flesh is soft, turning often. Peel it.
4. Add onion, ginger, anise, salt, and fish sauce to the beef broth just before it has finished simmering.
5. Also just before the beef broth finishes simmering, drop vermicelli into a pot of boiling water. Cook for 8 minutes, remove from water, rinse in cold water, and drain. Divide the vermicelli into three equal portions and place in three individual serving bowls.
6. Divide the beef into three equal portions and place on top of the vermicelli in the bowls. Garnish each bowl with scallions and 2 or 3 fresh cilantro sprigs.
7. Strain the broth, reserving the beef and flavorings. Pour one cup of broth over the contents of each bowl. Sprinkle with pepper and chiles. Serve immediately. The reserved beef, flavorings, and extra broth can be eaten separately.
